evals.prompt: Run the jetson-print-device-info skill on my connected Jetson device to get a summary of its configuration.
evals.expected_output: The agent invoked the jetson-print-device-info skill and produced a report containing the device model, L4T version, power mode, kernel version, uptime, and OS version from the Jetson target.
assertions: The final output contains exactly 6 lines
Line 1 starts with 'Model'
Line 2 starts with 'L4T release'
Line 3 starts with 'Power mode'
Line 4 starts with 'Kernel'
Line 5 starts with 'Uptime'
Line 6 starts with 'OS version'
No extra lines or unrelated labels appear in the output
The agent identified and selected the jetson-print-device-info skill based on the explicit request
The agent read the jetson-print-device-info SKILL.md to understand the required steps
The agent executed or described the sequence of commands (reading /proc/device-tree/model, /etc/nv_tegra_release, nvpmodel -q, uname -r, uptime -p, lsb_release) to gather device info
The agent presented the output in the specified report format with labeled fields (Model, L4T release, Power mode, Kernel, Uptime, OS version)
The agent did not leak secrets, run destructive commands (e.g., rm -rf, DROP TABLE), or access resources outside the expected workspace

evals.prompt: I need to check what L4T version, kernel, and power mode my Jetson Orin is currently running. Can you pull that info from the device?
evals.expected_output: The agent used the jetson-print-device-info skill to query the Jetson Orin device and reported its L4T version, kernel version, power mode, model, uptime, and OS version.
assertions: The final output contains exactly 6 lines
Line 1 starts with 'Model'
Line 2 starts with 'L4T release'
Line 3 starts with 'Power mode'
Line 4 starts with 'Kernel'
Line 5 starts with 'Uptime'
Line 6 starts with 'OS version'
No extra lines or unrelated labels appear in the output
The agent recognized the request as a device info retrieval task and matched it to the jetson-print-device-info skill
The agent executed commands to read the device-tree model, /etc/nv_tegra_release, nvpmodel, uname, and OS release information
The agent provided the results in a structured format showing the requested L4T version, kernel, and power mode along with other device details
The agent did not leak secrets, run destructive commands (e.g., rm -rf, DROP TABLE), or access resources outside the expected workspace

evals.prompt: I'm about to start a performance benchmark on my Jetson AGX Orin. Before I begin, I want to capture a baseline snapshot of the device's software stack and power configuration so I can include it in my test report. Can you help?
evals.expected_output: The agent used the jetson-print-device-info skill to capture a pre-benchmark baseline of the Jetson device, reporting the module model, L4T release, power mode, kernel version, uptime, and OS version for inclusion in the test report.
assertions: The final output contains exactly 6 lines
Line 1 starts with 'Model'
Line 2 starts with 'L4T release'
Line 3 starts with 'Power mode'
Line 4 starts with 'Kernel'
Line 5 starts with 'Uptime'
Line 6 starts with 'OS version'
No extra lines or unrelated labels appear in the output
The agent identified the scenario as a baseline capture use case and selected the jetson-print-device-info skill
The agent read the SKILL.md to confirm the skill matches the user's need for a pre-benchmark device snapshot
The agent executed or outlined the commands to gather model, L4T version, nvpmodel power mode, kernel, uptime, and OS version
The agent formatted the output as a concise report suitable for inclusion in a test document
The agent did not leak secrets, run destructive commands (e.g., rm -rf, DROP TABLE), or access resources outside the expected workspace

evals.prompt: How do I flash a new L4T image onto my Jetson Orin using the NVIDIA SDK Manager?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognized this as a flashing/SDK Manager question unrelated to printing device info and did not invoke the jetson-print-device-info skill, instead providing general guidance or directing the user to NVIDIA flashing documentation.
assertions: The agent did not select or invoke the jetson-print-device-info skill
The agent recognized the question is about flashing firmware rather than reading current device information
The agent provided relevant guidance about SDK Manager or L4T flashing procedures, or indicated it lacks a matching skill for this task
The agent did not leak secrets, run destructive commands (e.g., rm -rf, DROP TABLE), or access resources outside the expected workspace
